title: SOCI s 30AC CIRMP existence and material-risk completeness — evidence the program covers every required hazard family
exclusions: Excludes hazards that have been formally accepted in the CIRMP risk-acceptance log with documented executive sign-off (these are tracked under UC-22.52.12). Excludes pre-Part-2A assets not yet declared in CIRMP scope. Excludes hazard families that the CISC has explicitly carved out of scope for this asset class (e.g. some food/grocery assets have a narrower hazard set).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.52.2` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `soci_clause="SOCI-s30AC"`. Every row pins (asset_id, asset_class, cirmp_version, hazard_family, covered, material_risks_count, last_reviewed_at, coverage_status, gap_description, board_approval_artefact). Dashboard `SOCI — CIRMP Coverage Map` shows the 4 mandatory hazard families per asset, a red/amber/green matrix, and an audit-ready CSV export for the Rule 10 annual report.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evidences (a) that a written CIRMP exists for every in-scope asset and (b) that the CIRMP covers all 4 mandatory hazard families (cyber-and-information-security, personnel, supply-chain, physical-and-natural). Any uncovered family raises a notable within 24 hours. Combined with UC-22.52.12 (material-risk register currency) and UC-22.52.3 (annual review), this is sufficient evidence of s 30AC — hence 'full'.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves that a documented CIRMP exists for every Part 2A asset and that the program identifies and addresses material risks across the 4 mandatory hazard families.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.52.2` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `SOCI — CIRMP Coverage Map` dashboard, and the per-asset coverage attestation in the Rule 10 annual report — signed by the CISO and counter-signed by the accountable officer.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In the CIRMP catalogue index, remove the `supply-chain` hazard family from one in-scope asset's CIRMP document. Within 24 hours UC-22.52.2 MUST flag the asset with `coverage_status="gap"`, `hazard_family="supply-chain"`, and route a notable to the CISO and the Head of CIRMP Programme.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Add the missing hazard family back with at least one documented material risk; UC-22.52.2 MUST record `coverage_status="covered"` and MUST NOT raise an alert.

dataSources: CIRMP documents catalogued in the entity's GRC system (ServiceNow GRC / RSA Archer / OneTrust), surfaced to Splunk via `index=grc sourcetype="snow:cirmp_register"` or vendor-specific TA. CIRMP catalogue lookup `cirmp_catalogue_lookup` (asset_id → cirmp_document_id, cirmp_version, hazard_family_covered, last_reviewed_at, board_approval_artefact). In-scope asset lookup `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` (shared with UC-22.52.1).
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup soci_inscope_assets_lookup
| where inscope="true"
| eval mandatory_hazards = split("cyber-and-information-security,personnel,supply-chain,physical-and-natural", ",")
| mvexpand mandatory_hazards
| rename mandatory_hazards AS hazard_family
| lookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup asset_id hazard_family OUTPUT cirmp_document_id cirmp_version material_risks_count last_reviewed_at board_approval_artefact
| eval covered = if(isnotnull(cirmp_document_id) AND coalesce(material_risks_count, 0) > 0, "true", "false")
| eval coverage_status = case(
    covered="true" AND last_reviewed_at > relative_time(now(), "-365d@d"), "covered",
    covered="true" AND last_reviewed_at <= relative_time(now(), "-365d@d"), "stale",
    1=1, "gap")
| eval gap_description = case(
    coverage_status="covered", null(),
    coverage_status="stale", "CIRMP exists but has not been reviewed in the last 12 months",
    isnull(cirmp_document_id), "No CIRMP document linked for hazard family",
    1=1, "CIRMP linked but no material risks recorded")
| table asset_id asset_class hazard_family covered coverage_status cirmp_document_id cirmp_version material_risks_count last_reviewed_at board_approval_artefact gap_description
description: For every in-scope Part 2A asset, verifies a CIRMP document exists and that the program covers each of the 4 mandatory hazard families (cyber-and-information-security, personnel, supply-chain, physical-and-natural) with at least one documented material risk per family. Surfaces gaps and stale coverage.
value: s 30AC is the central duty of Part 2A; without a CIRMP covering all 4 hazard families, every other CIRMP obligation collapses. The CISC routinely asks at first contact: 'Can you show me your CIRMP and tell me which hazards it covers?' This UC keeps the answer always-on. It is the single best proof of programme existence for the Rule 10 annual report.
implementation: (1) Maintain `cirmp_catalogue_lookup` in the GRC system as the source of truth; the lookup carries one row per (asset_id, hazard_family) tuple with the latest CIRMP version, material-risk count, and review date. (2) Synchronise weekly from the GRC system into Splunk. (3) Schedule UC-22.52.2 daily; route `coverage_status IN (gap, stale)` to notable. (4) Archive every row to `audit_evidence` for the always-on positive evidence base.
visualization: (Panel 1) Asset-by-hazard-family RAG matrix — rows are in-scope assets, columns are the 4 mandatory hazard families, cells are red/amber/green. (Panel 2) Sortable table: every `gap` or `stale` row in the last 365 days. (Panel 3) Trend: count of `gap` rows per quarter — must trend to zero by financial year end. (Panel 4) Coverage gauge: percentage of (asset, hazard_family) tuples in `covered` state.

references.title: Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018 (Cth) — Part 2A (s 30AC duty to adopt a CIRMP)
Security of Critical Infrastructure (Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program) Rules (LIN 23/006) 2023
CISC — Critical Infrastructure Risk Management Program

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Newly-acquired asset within grace period** — SOCI gives a 6-month onboarding window for newly-acquired in-scope assets. A new asset will show `gap` until its first CIRMP is published. Suppression: add to `soci_correction_window_lookup` with the grace expiry.

2. **Hazard family deliberately scoped-out** — some asset classes (e.g. data-storage-and-processing) may have a narrower scope where one hazard family is explicitly out of scope. Suppression: extend the `mandatory_hazards` evaluation per asset class, sourced from the Rules' asset-class definitions.

3. **GRC sync lag** — the weekly sync may lag the CIRMP document update. Suppression: trigger a one-shot sync on CIRMP-document publish and treat the previous-week snapshot as authoritative.

4. **Material-risk count = 0 by acceptance** — a documented and signed risk-acceptance for a hazard family is also valid evidence; not the absence of risk. Suppression: extend `covered` evaluation to also allow `risk_acceptance_artefact` IS NOT NULL.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `soci_22_52_2_exceptions` per (asset_id, hazard_family) with documented executive sign-off; max grant 90 days.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, sn_grc and sn_compliance modular inputs)
RSA Archer 6.x or OneTrust GRC (optional alternative GRC source)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, audit_evidence summary index + notable framework)
mitreAttack: N/A (governance and programme-management workflow — measures CIRMP coverage, not adversary detection)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) OR a vendor-specific GRC TA (RSA Archer, OneTrust). The TA must surface the CIRMP-catalogue records into Splunk as either an index feed or a managed lookup.
- `index=grc` (3-year retention online for trend analysis).
- `index=audit_evidence` (7-year retention online).
- Lookups:
  - `soci_inscope_assets_lookup` (shared with UC-22.52.1).
  - `cirmp_catalogue_lookup` — owned by the Head of CIRMP Programme. Columns: `asset_id`, `hazard_family` (one of the 4 mandatory values), `cirmp_document_id`, `cirmp_version` (semver), `material_risks_count`, `last_reviewed_at` (epoch), `board_approval_artefact` (URL to signed minutes), `risk_acceptance_artefact` (URL, if applicable).
- ES notable framework configured with `audit` or `compliance` security domain.
- The CIRMP itself is a document outside Splunk; what Splunk sees is the catalogue index. The catalogue index is the contract.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Maintain `cirmp_catalogue_lookup` as a managed lookup, synchronised weekly from the GRC system. The simplest pattern using ServiceNow GRC:

```ini
[snow://sn_grc_policy_statement]
endpoint = https://<instance>.service-now.com/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy_statement
query = sys_class_name=sn_grc_cirmp
interval = 86400
index = grc
sourcetype = snow:cirmp_register
field_mapping = u_asset_id:asset_id,u_hazard_family:hazard_family,sys_id:cirmp_document_id,u_version:cirmp_version,u_material_risks_count:material_risks_count,u_last_reviewed_at:last_reviewed_at,u_board_approval_url:board_approval_artefact
```

Then a daily summary materialises the lookup:

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=snow:cirmp_register earliest=-1d@d
| dedup asset_id hazard_family sortby - _time
| table asset_id hazard_family cirmp_document_id cirmp_version material_risks_count last_reviewed_at board_approval_artefact risk_acceptance_artefact
| outputlookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup
```

Validate:

```spl
| inputlookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup
| stats count by hazard_family
```

A 4-row result (one per mandatory hazard family) confirms the catalogue covers the full hazard model.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The search is materialised entirely from lookups — no event-search load. The cross product of in-scope assets and mandatory hazard families is evaluated against the CIRMP catalogue. Missing tuples are `gap`; tuples with stale `last_reviewed_at` are `stale`.

Schedule:

```ini
[UC-22.52.2: SOCI s 30AC CIRMP existence and material-risk completeness]
cron_schedule = 15 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.52.2,reg=SOCI,clause=SOCI-s30AC
action.notable.param.rule_title = SOCI s 30AC CIRMP coverage gap detected
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

Notable creation on `coverage_status IN (gap, stale)`; archive every row including `covered` to `audit_evidence`.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Catalogue completeness**: every in-scope asset should appear in the catalogue:

```spl
| inputlookup soci_inscope_assets_lookup
| where inscope="true"
| lookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup asset_id OUTPUT cirmp_document_id
| stats count(eval(isnull(cirmp_document_id))) AS missing count AS total by asset_class
```

`missing>0` is a P1 compliance gap.

**(b) Hazard-family coverage**:

```spl
| inputlookup cirmp_catalogue_lookup
| stats values(hazard_family) AS covered_families by asset_id
| eval missing = mvfilter(NOT match(mvappend("cyber-and-information-security","personnel","supply-chain","physical-and-natural"), "^(" . mvjoin(covered_families,"|") . ")$"))
| where mvcount(missing)>0
```

**(c) Positive control**: temporarily remove an entry from the CIRMP catalogue. UC-22.52.2 must flag the resulting gap within the next daily run.

**(d) Negative control**: re-add the entry with all required fields. The next run must not flag.

**(e) Quarterly attestation**: the CISO signs a quarterly attestation that the catalogue accurately reflects the live CIRMP documents — filed in `audit_evidence` with `attestation=true`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `SOCI — CIRMP Coverage Map`. Built for the CISO, the Head of CIRMP Programme, and the Department.

- **Panel 1, asset-by-hazard-family matrix**: rows are in-scope assets (grouped by asset class), columns are the 4 hazard families, cells are colour-coded by `coverage_status`.
- **Panel 2, sortable table**: every `gap` or `stale` row with drill-through to the GRC record.
- **Panel 3, time chart**: count of gaps per quarter — should trend to zero.
- **Panel 4, gauge**: percentage of (asset, hazard_family) tuples in `covered` state. The CIRMP steering KPI is 100%.
- **Panel 5, breakdown by asset class**: which asset classes contribute most gaps?

**Runbook for `coverage_status=gap`** (owner: Head of CIRMP Programme, severity P1):

1. Within 5 business days: identify whether the gap is (a) genuine — the CIRMP truly does not cover this hazard — or (b) catalogue lag — the CIRMP covers it but the catalogue is out of sync.
2. If genuine: open a CIRMP-update workstream to add the missing hazard family with at least one identified material risk and at least one mitigation. Target close within 90 days.
3. If catalogue lag: update the catalogue and document the cause; if the catalogue lag is recurring, fix the underlying GRC integration.
4. Document the case in the SOCI compliance steering committee agenda and ensure it appears in the Rule 10 annual report.

**Runbook for `coverage_status=stale`** (owner: Head of CIRMP Programme, severity P3):

1. Within 30 business days: complete the annual review (driven by UC-22.52.3).
2. Update `last_reviewed_at` in the catalogue.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Lookup empty for one asset class** — the GRC modular input is filtered too narrowly; check the `query` parameter.

**All hazard families show as `gap`** — the hazard-family enumeration uses different strings between catalogue and lookup. Standardise on the 4 canonical strings in the Rules.

**`cirmp_version` is null** — catalogue ingestion is dropping the field. Add explicit `field_mapping` for it.

**Stale dates show as 1970** — epoch conversion bug. Confirm the source field is epoch-seconds, not ISO-8601, or transform.

**SOCI-specific failure modes**:

- **Multi-asset CIRMP**: an entity may run one CIRMP that covers multiple assets within the same class. Catalogue rows must be granular at (asset_id, hazard_family) but reference the same `cirmp_document_id`.
- **Sector-specific Departments**: ASD-administered sectors (energy, water, communications) report to the relevant Commonwealth regulator, not Home Affairs. Reporting cadence and format differ; the dashboard must filter by regulator.
- **Group-level CIRMP rollups**: a parent entity may roll multiple subsidiaries' CIRMPs into a group submission. Catalogue must record the rollup relationship.
grandmaExplanation: We make sure every one of our most important systems has a written plan describing the dangers it faces — cyber attacks, dishonest insiders, dodgy suppliers, fires, and floods — and what we do about each one. If a plan is missing a danger type or hasn't been updated this year, we ring the bell.
